concerns.text: Task T022 ('Implement dominant eigenvalue calculation... from global covariance matrix or per-sample approximation') violates the data-flow dependency on Constitution Principle VI and Spec FR-002. The spec requires the covariance matrix to be computed 'for every sample' to derive a per-sample entanglement score. A 'global covariance matrix' is a single artifact representing the whole dataset, not a per-sample feature. If T022 computes a global matrix, it cannot produce the per-sample features required by T024 (which reads 'aligned data' and writes 'features.json'). The task order implies a consumer (T024) waiting for a producer (T022) that may not produce the correct artifact shape (per-sample vs global). T022 must be strictly defined to compute the per-sample covariance matrix and its eigenvalue.

Task T027 ('Implement dimensional fidelity loss calculation... in code/features.py') creates a semantic ordering violation regarding artifact flow. FR-003 defines 'dimensional fidelity loss' as the target variable (Y), while FR-002 defines 'entanglement features' as the predictors (X). T027 places the target calculation inside the feature engineering module (`features.py`), which is semantically the producer of X. This conflates the production of the target artifact with the feature artifact. While not a hard 'consumer before producer' in time, it breaks the logical data-flow separation required for the training script (T028/T031) to correctly distinguish between inputs (features) and targets. The target calculation should be a distinct step or clearly separated in the pipeline flow before the training task consumes both.

Task T030 ('Implement evaluation script... permutation test p-value') conflicts with the spec's Success Criterion SC-002, which mandates a 'paired t-test' against a null baseline for MAE improvement. The task order implies the evaluation script will produce a 'permutation test p-value' (as per FR-005) but omits the 'paired t-test' required by SC-002. If T030 only implements the permutation test, the downstream validation (SC-002) cannot be satisfied. The task must be updated to include the paired t-test logic to ensure the artifact (evaluation results) contains the required metric before the project concludes.
